domain: Tahitian va'a outrigger canoe (asymmetric single ama on left with fixed iato lever arm; main hull flat outer/rounded inner side; counterbalance is single-mass not symmetric twin)
specific_mechanism: Single-sided ama counterbalance at fixed lever arm provides anti-roll stability for an otherwise asymmetric hull; stability emerges from a fixed lever distance to one auxiliary mass rather than bilateral symmetry.
llm_application: AMA-COUNTERBALANCE: in an H-head transformer, designate one head per layer as a frozen 'counterbalance head' initialized to a single known direction (e.g., positional shift identity or a top-PCA-eigendirection of the layer's residual stream from a calibration set). The remaining H-1 heads are free. Add a variance-triggered regularizer R(x) = max(0, var_t(residual_layer) - τ)^2 · |proj_anchor(active_head_outputs)|^2 — i.e., penalize the projection of active heads onto the anchor direction ONLY when residual-stream variance exceeds a learned threshold τ. This differs from (a) anchor-token attention (anchors are over tokens not heads), (b) TensorLLM Tucker shared-subspace (couples all heads symmetrically), (c) GQA (head-clusters share keys symmetrically) by (i) a single fixed-direction anchor head, (ii) variance-triggered (not always-on) regularization, (iii) asymmetric lever-arm coupling (one anchor head, not pairs or groups).
novelty_claim: Stable-Attention bounds head variance symmetrically. TensorLLM Tucker couples all heads. Anchor-token attention uses token anchors. GQA shares keys symmetrically. AMA-COUNTERBALANCE recombines (a) ONE-HEAD asymmetric anchor + (b) VARIANCE-TRIGGERED activation of regularizer + (c) FIXED-DIRECTION counterbalance head. Three known components combined; not a wholly new artefact.
